info.name: BX Finance — 3-Token Delegated Chain (may_act / RFC 8693)
info.description:
Tests the full 3-token delegation chain:

1. **Subject Token** — obtained via Authorization Code + PKCE using Postman's built-in OAuth2 helper. Carries `may_act.sub = PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`.
2. **MCP Token** — RFC 8693 Token Exchange #1. Banking App exchanges the Subject Token for an MCP-scoped token.
3. **PingOne API Token** — Client Credentials from BX Finance MCP Service. Used to call the PingOne Management API.
4. **User Lookup** — GET /users/{sub} using Token 3, proving the full chain works end-to-end.

## Setup
Import this collection, then set the following **Collection Variables** (gear icon → Edit → Variables):

| Variable | Where to find it |
|---|---|
| `PINGONE_ENVIRONMENT_ID` | PingOne Console → Environment → Settings |
| `PINGONE_REGION` | `com` (US) or `eu` / `ca` / `ap` |
| `PINGONE_CORE_USER_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance User** app |
| `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance Banking App** |
| `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_SECRET` | Client Secret of **BX Finance Banking App** |
| `MCP_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance MCP Service** |
| `MCP_CLIENT_SECRET` | Client Secret of **BX Finance MCP Service** |

## Step 1 note
PingOne does not support ROPC. Step 1 uses Postman's built-in **Authorization Code + PKCE** OAuth2 helper. You must add `https://oauth.pstmn.io/v1/callback` as a Redirect URI on the BX Finance User app before clicking Get New Access Token.

All token variables (`subject_token`, `mcp_token`, `pingone_api_token`, `user_sub`) are **auto-populated** by the test scripts as you run each request in order.

variable.description: Client ID — BX Finance User app
Client ID — BX Finance Banking App
Client Secret — BX Finance Banking App
Client ID — BX Finance MCP Service
Client Secret — BX Finance MCP Service
AUTO — set by Step 1 test script after PKCE login
AUTO — set by Step 1 (user PingOne UUID from sub claim)

request.description:
Obtains the Subject Token (Token 1) via **Authorization Code + PKCE**.

## How to get the token

1. Click the **Authorization** tab on this request.
2. Scroll to the bottom and click **Get New Access Token**.
3. A browser window opens — log in as your test user.
4. Postman receives the callback and shows the token. Click **Use Token**.
5. Run the request — the test script decodes the access token, saves it to `subject_token`, and validates `may_act`.

## Required setup in PingOne
Add `https://oauth.pstmn.io/v1/callback` as a **Redirect URI** on the **BX Finance User** app (Configuration tab) so PingOne accepts the Postman callback. Remove it after testing if desired.

## The `resource` parameter and `aud`
Postman's OAuth2 helper does not expose a `resource` parameter field. To get `aud = https://ai-agent.pingdemo.com` on the access token you have two options:

**Option A (recommended):** After clicking **Get New Access Token**, Postman will show the raw token. Copy the `access_token` value, paste it into Collection Variables as `subject_token`, then run just the test script by sending this request with that token in the Authorization header.

**Option B:** Manually build the authorize URL with `&resource=https://ai-agent.pingdemo.com` appended, complete the flow in a browser, exchange the code yourself using the token request below, and paste the resulting `access_token` into `subject_token`.

The `may_act` claim will appear only if:
- The user has `mayAct = { "sub": "<PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID>" }` set (run **Utility — Set mayAct** first)
- The `BX Finance User` app has the `may_act` Attribute Mapping (Step 2a of setup guide)
- `banking:agent:invoke` scope is on the app's Resources tab

exec: // After completing the PKCE flow via Get New Access Token,
// Postman sets the token on the request Authorization header.
// Extract it here and save to the collection variable.
console.log('[Step 1 pre-request] subject_token captured from Authorization header');
console.warn('[Step 1] No Bearer token on request — did you click Get New Access Token and Use Token?');
// The userinfo endpoint returns the user profile — 200 confirms the token is valid.
pm.test('Step 1: userinfo 200 — Subject Token valid', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
console.error('[Step 1] subject_token not set. Complete the PKCE flow: Authorization tab → Get New Access Token → Use Token, then re-send.');
console.log('[Step 1] Subject Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
console.warn('[Step 1] WARNING: may_act missing. Check user mayAct attribute and BX Finance User app Attribute Mapping.');
console.error('[Step 1] Could not decode JWT payload:', e.message);

request.description:
RFC 8693 Token Exchange #1.

The **BX Finance Banking App** (`PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`) exchanges the Subject Token for a narrower MCP-scoped token.

PingOne validates:
- `subject_token.may_act.sub` === `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` (the Banking App's own client ID)

On success the returned MCP Token will have:
- `aud = https://mcp-server.pingdemo.com`
- `act.sub = PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`
- `scope = banking:accounts:read banking:transactions:read banking:transactions:write`

If you get `invalid_grant`: the `may_act.sub` on the user's Subject Token doesn't match `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`. Fix by setting `mayAct = { "sub": "<PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID>" }` on the user record (demo app → /demo-data → Enable may_act).

exec: console.log('[Step 2] MCP Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
// Verify sub preserved from Subject Token
pm.test('Step 2: sub preserved from Subject Token', () => {
console.error('[Step 2] Could not decode JWT payload:', e.message);

request.description:
Client Credentials grant for **BX Finance MCP Service**.

This is NOT a token exchange. The MCP Service authenticates with its own credentials and requests a scoped token for the PingOne Management API.

The returned token will have:
- `aud = https://api.pingone.com`
- `scope = p1:read:user p1:update:user`
- No `sub` (it represents the service, not a user)

If `invalid_scope`: enable `p1:read:user` and `p1:update:user` on the BX Finance MCP Service app Resources tab (Step 2c of setup guide).

exec: console.log('[Step 3] PingOne API Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
// Token 3 should NOT have a sub (service token, not user token)
console.warn('[Step 3] Unexpected sub claim on PingOne API Token:', payload.sub);
console.error('[Step 3] Could not decode JWT payload:', e.message);

request.description:
Calls the PingOne Management API using Token 3 (PingOne API Token).

`user_sub` was captured automatically in Step 1 from the Subject Token's `sub` claim.

A 200 response with the user object confirms the full chain works end-to-end:
1. User logged in → Subject Token with `may_act`
2. Banking App exchanged → MCP Token with `act`
3. MCP Service obtained → PingOne API Token with `p1:read:user`
4. Management API authorized the call

Also verifies that the `mayAct` custom attribute is present on the user record.

exec: pm.test('Step 4: Status 200 — user lookup succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
// Check mayAct custom attribute is set
console.log('[Step 4] mayAct on user record:', JSON.stringify(json.mayAct));
console.warn('[Step 4] WARNING: mayAct attribute missing from user record. Token Exchange will fail at runtime. Set it via /demo-data → Enable may_act or via PATCH /users/{id}.');

urlencoded.description: Replace with any of: subject_token, mcp_token, pingone_api_token

request.description:
Introspects any saved token against PingOne's introspection endpoint.

Change the `token` body value to one of:
- `{{subject_token}}` — Token 1
- `{{mcp_token}}` — Token 2
- `{{pingone_api_token}}` — Token 3

Or use the [PingIdentity JWT Decoder](https://developer.pingidentity.com/en/tools/jwt-decoder.html) to decode offline.

exec: console.warn('[Introspect] Token is INACTIVE or expired.');

request.description:
Sets the `mayAct` custom attribute on the test user.

**Run this if Step 1 returns a Subject Token without a `may_act` claim** (the test script will warn you).

Requires Token 3 (`pingone_api_token`) in the Authorization header — run Steps 1–3 first.

`PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` is the client ID UUID of **BX Finance Banking App** — the app that is authorized to exchange this user's token.

exec: console.log('[Set mayAct] Now re-run Step 1 to get a fresh Subject Token with the may_act claim.');
